Function-by-function guides

How to Use Claude
in Your Business.

Generic "AI for business" content is useless. What matters is what Claude does inside the specific function you're trying to improve. These guides walk through the actual workflows — what to deploy, in what order, with what prompts — for seven core business functions.

Sales
How to Use Claude for Sales
Prospect research, account briefs, sequence personalization, call prep, follow-up generation. The Claude workflows that compress a rep's prep time from 90 minutes to 10.
Read the guide →
Marketing
How to Use Claude for Marketing
Content production, campaign briefs, ICP research, messaging frameworks, repurposing. Where Claude amplifies a small marketing team without flattening the voice.
Read the guide →
Customer service
How to Use Claude for Customer Service
Ticket triage, response drafting, knowledge base authoring, escalation routing. Cut average handle time without losing the human voice in your replies.
Read the guide →
Operations
How to Use Claude for Operations
SOP authoring, process documentation, vendor management, internal comms. The operational workflows that finally get written when Claude does the first draft.
Read the guide →
Finance
How to Use Claude for Finance
Board-deck prep, variance analysis narration, vendor review, policy authoring. Where Claude replaces the painful "make this make sense" step in finance work.
Read the guide →
HR & People
How to Use Claude for HR
Job descriptions, interview kits, performance review drafts, onboarding docs, handbook updates. The Claude workflows that make a one-person People team feel like five.
Read the guide →
Project management
How to Use Claude for Project Management
Status reports, scope documents, risk logs, retrospectives. The Claude workflows for PMs who need the documentation done but don't want to write it from scratch.
Read the guide →

Want the prompts that power these?

Most of these guides reference our system-prompt library — twenty production-grade prompts for the functions above. Each one is annotated with the structure, the failure modes, and how to customize it for your business.

Open the Prompts Library → Learn the prompt-writing method
